objective: Fix both reference implementations for Course 4 Lab 4.5 so a transformer block counts two LayerNorm components, each with a trainable gamma and beta vector: use `2 * parameter_count_layer_norm(hyperparams)` (equivalently `4 * embedding_dim`) instead of `2 * embedding_dim`, and add or update a focused regression check.
completion_criteria: The notebook and packaged feedback reference must agree that one LayerNorm owns gamma and beta vectors of length `embedding_dim` and that the block constructs two LayerNorms. Test several embedding sizes and assert `block = attention + MLP + 2 * LayerNorm` in both copies.
non_results: Finite computation outside its stated range is not a proof of a universal claim.
A candidate is not a solution until independent and human review are recorded.
Restating a known reduction, checking examples, or giving an unproved key lemma is not terminal success.

baseline_review.requirement: Audit exact statement/status, prior work, established facts, ruled-out routes, open leads, tools, and acceptance path before technical research.

synthesis_summary: Prepared a review-ready patch against upstream e37bc994 that corrects both references and adds a focused two-copy regression test. Baseline, one-copy controls, clean application, notebook JSON, compilation, regression, and independent checks behaved exactly as predicted. Temporary upstream clones were removed after replay; only durable patch, checker, experiments, receipt, state, and checkpoint remain.

established_facts.claim: One modeled LayerNorm has two trainable vectors of length embedding_dim.
established_facts.evidence: The upstream helper returns embedding_dim + embedding_dim and documents gamma plus beta.

established_facts.claim: The unmodified block references are short by 2 * embedding_dim.
established_facts.evidence: Baseline checker observed deficits 512 at embedding_dim=256 and 256 at embedding_dim=128 in both copies.

established_facts.claim: The exact patch passes both semantic validators.
established_facts.scope: Patched e37bc994 and the tested fixtures.

strategies.family: two-copy source correction with independent semantic regression
strategies.title: Two-copy arithmetic repair, focused three-fixture regression test, clean patch replay, independent fixed-oracle checker, and one-copy negative controls.
strategies.mechanism: Replace each undercount with twice the existing LayerNorm helper, test both shipped references compositionally, and independently compare them with fixed numeric oracles.
strategies.hypothesis: Changing both transformer-block references from 2 * embedding_dim to 2 * parameter_count_layer_norm(hyperparams) makes each equal attention + MLP + two gamma/beta LayerNorms for several embedding dimensions.
strategies.discriminating_test: Run both references at embedding dimensions 1, 128, and 256, requiring LayerNorm = 2 * embedding_dim and block = attention + MLP + 2 * LayerNorm; then replay fixed totals 461440 and 198272 with a separately written checker.
strategies.blocker: External submission and maintainer acceptance require human authorization and coordination.
strategies.reopen_condition: Retry implementation work only if current upstream no longer accepts the patch, a reviewer finds a test or style defect, or an equivalent change alters the required scope.

open_leads.description: Human review and upstream pull-request submission.
open_leads.rationale: The patch and validators are complete; maintainer review is the remaining acceptance path.
open_leads.next_experiment: Recheck upstream head and replay git apply --check plus both validators.

open_leads.rationale: Useful as integration assurance but not necessary to discriminate the arithmetic correction.
open_leads.next_experiment: Execute the patched notebook in its supported environment and retain the run log.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Repair only the packaged feedback reference.
ruled_out.scope: The exact packaged-only change at e37bc994.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: The notebook reference is removed or independently corrected upstream.

ruled_out.scope: The exact notebook-only change at e37bc994.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: The packaged reference is removed or independently corrected upstream.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Rely on the existing learner-feedback test as independent validation.
ruled_out.reason: It derives expected values from the same reference implementation, so a wrong reference remains self-consistent.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: The learner test adopts a direct or materially independent oracle.

next_session.objective: Obtain human review and, if authorized, submit a current-head-validated upstream pull request.
next_session.stop_condition: Stop or redirect if issue #37 is closed, an equivalent pull request exists, or upstream already passes the two-copy checker.

tactical_memory.current_bottleneck: Technical completion is achieved locally; human review, current-head replay, and upstream acceptance remain.

failure_signatures.signature: The existing learner-feedback test obtains expected values from the same reference implementation, allowing a coordinated reference error to remain self-consistent.

constraints_learned.constraint: Both reference copies must change together.
constraints_learned.evidence: Both one-copy negative controls exited 1 on the unchanged copy.

constraints_learned.constraint: One LayerNorm contributes gamma and beta vectors, hence 2 * embedding_dim parameters.
constraints_learned.scope: The LayerNorm model explicitly documented by both course references.
constraints_learned.evidence: Existing parameter_count_layer_norm definition and focused direct-oracle assertions.

decision_history.prediction: The unmodified references would both be short by 2 * embedding_dim, either one-copy repair would leave exactly the other copy failing, and the complete patch would pass.
decision_history.observation: Baseline failed both copies by 512 at embedding_dim=256; each one-copy control left the other copy failing by 512; the complete patch passed all focused and independent checks.
decision_history.next_discriminator: Immediately before submission, recheck upstream main and replay the patch and both validators.

prior_art_decisions.exact_delta: The current upstream sources and issue identify the defect but contain no patch or independent two-copy regression; this artifact changes both references and adds that regression.
prior_art_decisions.duplicate_risk: A new upstream commit or unindexed pull request could independently land the same two-line correction after the 2026-07-22 status check.
prior_art_decisions.comparison_test: Recheck issue state, upstream main, and repository pull requests before submission.
